name: Gnuplot
description: A lightweight, command-line plotting program for creating 2D and 3D plots from data files and mathematical functions. A classic tool in physics for quick data visualisation.
whyUseIt: Gnuplot is fast, scriptable, and runs everywhere. It is ideal for quick exploratory plots during analysis and can produce publication-quality output. Many physicists use it for its simplicity and speed when they do not need a full programming environment.
fieldPosition: Universal — quick data visualisation and exploration across all branches
physicistWorkflow: Load a data file, plot it with one command, fit a function, and export to PDF for inclusion in a paper.
supervisorExpects: Ability to produce clean, labelled plots quickly from the command line.
computationalImpact: Enables rapid data exploration without the overhead of a full programming environment.

vocabulary.definition: The output device — screen, PDF, PostScript, PNG, etc.
Gnuplot's built-in Levenberg-Marquardt nonlinear curve fitting

beginnerMistakes: Not setting proper axis ranges and labels
Forgetting to set the output terminal before plotting to file
